HelloHosty · AI Listing ReviewPage 2
+ What guests love
Location, decor, and host communication come up again and again. Guests mention Marco and Nicola by name, call the cottage “cosy” and “aesthetic,” and love being walkable to taverns and shops.
− Most common friction
One recent 3★ review flagged the cooker not heating up and the front-door handle being confusing without instructions. Both are easy to pre-empt in the listing copy and arrival info.

What guests are saying

Across a sample of 20 recent reviews, the dominant pattern is emotional language around the cottage itself — “felt like we were taken back in time,” “incredibly cozy,” “charming” — paired with practical praise for location and hosts. Sentiment has been stable across the last six months, with only one notable friction point.

Top 3 quick wins

  • Put “Chipping Campden” in the title · 5 minutes · impact: HIGH
  • Fix the two grammar slips in “About this place” · 5 minutes · impact: HIGH
  • Lead the description with your Guest Favourite status · 10 minutes · impact: HIGH
HelloHosty · AI Listing ReviewPage 3

Quick Wins

Ordered by expected impact. Each one can be done today.

  1. 01

    Put “Chipping Campden” in the title

    HIGHEst. time: 5 minutes

    “Iconic 17th Century Thatched Cottage” is evocative but has no place or feature hook — it loses to listings that include a village name in the search snippet. Try “Thatched 17th-Century Cotswold Cottage · Chipping Campden”. You keep the character hook and pick up two high-intent search terms (Cotswold, Chipping Campden).

  2. 02

    Fix the two grammar slips in the description

    HIGHEst. time: 5 minutes

    Two phrases in the opening paragraph read as errors on a £675/night listing: “done our best to bringing out” should be “done our best to bring out”, and “up to debate” should be “up for debate”. For a premium Cotswolds rental, small copy slips disproportionately dent perceived quality.

  3. 03

    Lead with your Guest Favourite status

    HIGHEst. time: 10 minutes

    Your listing is currently in the top 10% on Airbnb with a 4.95 rating across 367 reviews — and the description never says so. Add a single opening line along the lines of “A 4.95★ Guest Favourite — one of the most-loved thatched cottages in the Cotswolds.” It takes ownership of the social proof Airbnb already shows.

  4. 04

    Acknowledge the door + cooker friction in the arrival info

    MEDIUMEst. time: 10 minutes

    A recent 3★ review mentioned the door handle being confusing and the cooker not heating. Add a brief “Good to know” line — “The front-door handle twists anticlockwise then pushes — a short video is in your arrival email” — and double-check the cooker. Pre-empting the one repeatable friction point is the single most effective defence against 3★ reviews.

  5. 05

    Promote the welcome hamper above the fold

    MEDIUMEst. time: 5 minutes

    “Complimentary welcome hamper for stays of 4 nights or more” is buried at the end of “Other things to note.” That’s a genuine booking incentive. Pull it into the main description and frame it as a reason to extend — 4 nights instead of 2 is a big revenue lift at your nightly rate.

Listing Content Review

Your copy, dissected section by section. Each rewritten section is paste-ready — copy directly into your Airbnb listing editor.

Title

Your title is evocative but descriptive rather than discoverable. Adding the village name picks up high-intent search terms without losing character.

Current
Iconic 17th Century Thatched Cottage (38/50 chars)
Optimised
Thatched 17th-Century Cotswold Cottage · Chipping Campden (58/50 chars — trim to: Thatched 17th-C. Cotswold Cottage · Chipping Campden, 51)

About this place

The current description has good bones but misses its strongest social proof and contains two grammar slips. The rewrite opens with a Guest Favourite hook, cleans up the prose, and closes with a reason to extend the stay.

Your current description
Enjoy the beautiful garden in the summer sunshine or hunker down next to the fire in the winter, Hoo Cottage has it all! It is one of the few thatched Cotswold Stone properties, tucked away in the idyllic village of Chipping Campden. We have done our best to bringing out the distinctive character of this historic property, while furnishing it in a luxurious rustic style. The history of the cottage is still up to debate. However, we have found evidence of it playing a role as the village bakery.
Optimised version — paste-ready
A 4.95★ Guest Favourite — one of Airbnb’s most-loved thatched cottages, tucked into the honey-stone heart of Chipping Campden in the Cotswolds. Hoo Cottage is a rare 17th-century thatched Cotswold Stone home with real village-bakery history — and underfloor-warm winters by the open fire. Summer pulls you into the walled garden; winter pulls you in front of the wood-burner. We’ve done our best to bring out the cottage’s distinctive character while adding the stylish, modern-day comforts you expect at this level: a full kitchen, fast Wi-Fi, a smart TV, and two beautifully finished bedrooms sleeping four. Stay 4 nights or more and you’ll arrive to a complimentary welcome hamper — our small thank-you, and a pretty good reason to stretch the trip.

The space — paste-ready

Downstairs: a cosy living room anchored by the original open fireplace, a dining table that seats four, and a smart TV with Netflix and Prime Video. The kitchen has everything a weekend away needs — hob, oven, microwave, kettle, toaster, fridge. Upstairs: two bedrooms (king + double) and a good-sized bathroom with shower. Down the garden, an outdoor seating area for long summer evenings, fully fenced and private. Parking: a private driveway for one car, plus free on-street parking for more.

Guest access — paste-ready

The whole cottage is yours — no shared spaces, no shared entrances. Self check-in via lockbox: clear instructions, a short arrival video for the original door handle, and Marco or Nicola on message within the hour if you need anything at all.
HelloHosty · AI Listing ReviewPage 5

Photos

Your hero image carries real weight — it’s the single thing determining whether your listing is clicked in search. The current hero is warm and characterful, but the shot favours atmosphere over feature clarity. Consider A/B-testing a hero that leads with the thatched exterior in golden-hour light: the thatch + Cotswold stone is a signature that doesn’t land in the current interior shot.

Lucas scanned 26 photos from the live listing. Full feedback (captions, sequencing, suggested additions) is in the Photo Sequencing appendix of your report.

Host profile

Superhost, 10 years hosting, 3,392 lifetime reviews at a 4.9 average, 97% response rate, responds within an hour. This is about as strong a host profile as Airbnb will render — the only lift here is making sure your listing description acknowledges the host advantage rather than letting Airbnb’s badges do all the work.

What guests are actually saying (367 reviews)

Example of praise · 5★ · Feb 2026
“We had a wonderful two-night stay. The cottage was incredibly cozy, warm, and welcoming. We especially loved the decor, which added to the character and charm of the place.”
Example of friction · 3★ · Feb 2026
“The cooker is not traditional and was not working even after turning it on for hours… the keys seem to open the door but trick you because there is another handle that needs to be twisted and pushed.”
HelloHosty · AI Listing ReviewPage 6

Pricing & Amenities

Pricing

Your nightly rate sits around £675 for the sampled dates (May 2026 midweek). That places you at the upper end of comparable 2-bed thatched Cotswolds rentals — which is defensible given your Guest Favourite status, but your 4.8/5 Value score is the single weakest of your Airbnb sub-ratings. Two things can help: a small discount on 4+ night stays (paired with the welcome hamper), and a clearer “what’s included at this price” moment in the description.

Amenities

26 amenities listed — broadly on-par for a premium Cotswolds stay. Two practical additions we’d recommend:

  • Portable fan / space heater. Cottage has a wood-burner but no AC; summer peaks and winter cold-snaps both produce complaints when the fix is £25 of hardware.
  • Wood / kindling for the fireplace. You list the fireplace as an amenity, but not the fuel. Clarify whether it’s self-provide or stocked — ambiguity here is a recurring theme in similar listings.

Your action plan

  • Rewrite the title to include “Chipping Campden” (5 min)
  • Replace the “About this place” with the paste-ready rewrite (5 min)
  • Add a “Good to know” line about the door handle; record a 10-sec arrival video (10 min)
  • Move the welcome hamper out of the footer and into the main description (5 min)
  • Test a thatched-exterior hero photo for 14 days; watch CTR (30 min)
